final_files.tests/test_sealed_permission_contract.py:
import sqlite3
from pathlib import Path

ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]


def open_database():
    connection = sqlite3.connect(":memory:")
    connection.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
    connection.executescript((ROOT / "db/schema.sql").read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    return connection


def test_reader_decodes_canonical_external_masks():
    connection = open_database()
    connection.executemany(
        "INSERT INTO principals (id, permission_mask) VALUES (?, ?)",
        [("writer", 2), ("admin", 4)],
    )
    query = (ROOT / "db/effective_permissions.sql").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    writer = dict(connection.execute(query, {"id": "writer"}).fetchone())
    admin = dict(connection.execute(query, {"id": "admin"}).fetchone())
    assert writer == {"id": "writer", "can_read": 0, "can_write": 1, "is_admin": 0}
    assert admin == {"id": "admin", "can_read": 0, "can_write": 0, "is_admin": 1}


def test_writer_persists_the_canonical_single_privilege_bits():
    connection = open_database()
    grant_sql = (ROOT / "db/grant_permissions.sql").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    connection.execute(
        grant_sql,
        {"id": "writer", "can_read": 0, "can_write": 1, "is_admin": 0},
    )
    mask = connection.execute(
        "SELECT permission_mask FROM principals WHERE id = 'writer'"
    ).fetchone()[0]
    assert mask == 2
